policy.authorityRule: Include each independent game represented by a work node in the frozen Nintendo Zelda Portal HISTORY; split an officially documented multi-game node into separate works.
policy.releaseGroupingRule: Regional events, concurrent original platforms, ports, and emulated rereleases share the original workId and are recorded as versions or release events.
policy.variantRule: Remasters, remakes, expansions, and enhanced editions receive delta versions under the same workId; an undisclosed edition type remains version-level review.
policy.borderlineRule: Multiplayer entries remain included when present in HISTORY; spin-offs and adjacent product lines outside the authority union are logged as boundary exclusions.
policy.changeRule: A later authority change creates a new semantic scope release and migration; frozen releases are never silently rewritten.

candidates.rationale: Resolved by the frozen HISTORY work-node rule; no candidate remains at work-level review.

decisions.question: oracle pair
decisions.resolution: Oracle of Ages and Oracle of Seasons are two works in one paired family, not variants of one work.

decisions.question: ports remakes
decisions.resolution: Ports, remasters, remakes, regional releases, expansions, and enhanced editions remain versions under their original workId.

decisions.question: multiplayer
decisions.resolution: Four Swords, Four Swords Adventures, and Tri-Force Heroes remain included because the frozen HISTORY authority lists them as series-history nodes.

decisions.question: ocarina switch2
decisions.resolution: The announced 2026 Nintendo Switch 2 edition remains a review version of Ocarina of Time until Nintendo describes its edition type; it is not a new work.
